title: schoenus nitens subsp. krausei
intro: Schoenus nitens subsp. krausei, also known as the Krause's Bog Sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Schoenus nitens subsp. krausei include relief from coughs, congestion, and other respiratory symptoms, as well as its potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and can be found in combination with other herbs in traditional medicines and remedies.
